title: leucas argyrophylla
intro: Leucas argyrophylla, also known as the "Wild Jasmine", is a flowering plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leucas argyrophylla include its potential to help manage fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are commonly used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions that are used to treat various health conditions.
